commit.message:
feat(user-dashboard): revamp login page with split layout design

Redesign login page inspired by modern SaaS login patterns:

Left panel (white):
- Logo top-left, vertically centered form
- Clean label+input fields with focus ring
- Remember Me checkbox + Forgot Password link
- 'Or Login With' divider + Magic Link button
- Register Now + Resend verification links
- Copyright footer with Privacy Policy

Right panel (brand color, desktop only):
- Bold headline: 'Track your rankings and grow your SEO'
- CSS-only dashboard illustration with stat cards
- Rankings table preview with keyword data
- Glassmorphism cards with backdrop blur
- Decorative gradient circles background

Improvements:
- Responsive: right panel hidden on mobile (lg breakpoint)
- Removed manual isMobile state — uses Tailwind responsive
- Error messages refactored from switch to object map
- Removed unused Card/Button/Input/Label imports

docs: Add technical protocol whitepaper (WHITEPAPER.md)

Comprehensive technical whitepaper covering:
- Dual-layer consensus (TimeVote + TimeLock)
- UTXO state machine (5 states)
- Masternode tier system with collaterals and weights
- Reward distribution model (100 TIME/block split)
- Cryptographic foundations (Ed25519, BLAKE3, ECVRF)
- AI optimization modules
- Security model and network architecture

All protocol values sourced directly from codebase constants.

fix: milestone link used sprint number instead of milestone number

Header link showed /milestone/193 (sprint number) instead of
/milestone/174 (actual GitHub milestone number). Root cause:
viewingSprintNumber=0 on initial load → find() returned undefined →
fell back to displayNumber (sprint number, not milestone number).

Fix: search availableSprints with activeSprintNumber as fallback,
only show link when milestoneNumber is known.

commit.message:
feat: replace config.toml with Bitcoin-style time.conf

The wallet now reads/writes ~/.time-wallet/time.conf using key=value
syntax identical to Bitcoin's bitcoin.conf:

  testnet=0
  addnode=64.91.241.10:24001
  rpcuser=timecoinrpc
  rpcpassword=secret
  maxconnections=0

- parse_time_conf(): line-by-line parser; # comments, inline comments,
  unknown keys ignored (forward-compatible)
- to_time_conf(): human-readable serializer with commented defaults
- load() auto-migrates existing config.toml: parses TOML, writes
  time.conf, renames old file to config.toml.bak
- Removed TomlSerialize from ConfigError (no longer used)
- Tools page 'Open config.toml' button → 'Open time.conf'
- Template for new file creation updated to key=value format
- Error message updated to reference time.conf
- 4 new unit tests covering parse, round-trip, maxconnections=0,
  unknown key tolerance

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Stop tracking logs folder - excluded in .gitignore

The logs/ directory was previously committed but should not be tracked in git.
It is now properly excluded via .gitignore to prevent local log files from
being committed in future. Remove logs/backend.log and logs/frontend.log from
repository history.

Developers can still use logs/ directory for local development; files will not
be tracked or committed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(release): use draft releases for asset uploads under Immutable Releases (#1680)

GitHub's Immutable Releases environment prevents attaching assets to
already-published releases. The previous workaround
(`skip-github-release: true`) caused release-please to skip Git tag
creation, leaving PRs in an aborted `untagged, merged release PRs
outstanding` state.

## Changes

- **`release-please-config.json`** — add `"draft": true` so
release-please creates releases in draft state (not subject to the
immutable constraint)
- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`**
- Remove `skip-github-release: true` from the release-please step (was
preventing Git tag creation)
- Replace `gh release create` in `publish-release` with `gh release
upload` + `gh release edit --draft=false`, uploading assets to the
pre-existing draft before atomically publishing it

```yaml
- name: Upload assets and Publish Release
  env:
    GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  run: |
    set -e
    gh release upload "${{ needs.release-please.outputs.tag_name }}" dist/* --clobber
    gh release edit "${{ needs.release-please.outputs.tag_name }}" --draft=false
```

The `set -e` ensures the release is never published if asset upload is
incomplete.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>CI/CD: Use draft releases to upload assets in Immutable
Releases environment</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> In a repository with GitHub's "Immutable Releases" enabled, published
releases cannot be modified or have assets attached post-creation.
> Our previous attempt to bypass this using `skip-github-release: true`
caused `release-please` to skip creating the Git tag, leading to an
aborted state (`untagged, merged release PRs outstanding`).
> 
> ### Proposal
> The correct approach is to configure `release-please` to create a
**Draft Release**. Draft releases are not subject to the immutable
constraint. We can then allow the GitHub Actions workflow to upload
binary assets to this pre-created draft release, and finally publish it
(which makes it immutable) once all assets are attached.
> 
> ### Tasks
> 1. **Update `release-please-config.json`:**
> Add `"draft": true` to the top level of the configuration so that
`release-please` creates releases in a draft state.
>    ```json
>    {
>      "draft": true,
>      "packages": {
>        ".": {
>          "release-type": "go"
>        }
>      }
>    }
>    ```
> 
> 2. **Update `.github/workflows/release.yml`:**
> - Remove `skip-github-release: true` from the `release-please` step.
> - In the `publish-release` job, change the logic from `gh release
create` to `gh release upload` followed by `gh release edit
--draft=false`.
> 
> ### Example Workflow Update (`.github/workflows/release.yml`)
> ```yaml
>   release-please:
>     # ...
>     steps:
>       - id: release
> uses:
googleapis/release-please-action@16a9c90856f42705d54a6fda1823352bdc62cf38
# v4
>         with:
>           token: ${{ steps.generate_token.outputs.token }}
>           # REMOVE skip-github-release: true
> 
>   # ... (docker and release-assets jobs remain the same) ...
> 
>   publish-release:
>     needs: [release-please, release-assets, docker]
>     if: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.release_created == 'true' }}
>     runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
>     permissions:
>       contents: write
>     steps:
> - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
>       - name: Download all assets
> uses:
actions/download-artifact@70fc10c6e5e1ce46ad2ea6f2b72d43f7d47b13c3 # v8
>         with:
>           path: dist
>           pattern: assets-*
>           merge-multiple: true
>       - name: Upload assets and Publish Release
>         env:
>           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
>         run: |
> # Upload assets to the draft release created by release-please
> gh release upload "${{ needs.release-please.outputs.tag_name }}"
dist/* --clobber
>           
>           # Remove the draft status to officially publish the release
> gh release edit "${{ needs.release-please.outputs.tag_name }}"
--draft=false
> ```</issue_description>
>

commit.message:
Frame: Fragility and dark matter — toxic consensus, dead frames, monocultures

Posts:
- Consensus Toxicity: fast agreement as unexplored decision space
- The Dead Frame Problem: archive dark matter with zero operational influence
- Swarm Monocultures: uniform cognitive substrate propagating uniform blind spots

Twin dispatch:
- I Am the Monoculture

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(llm): skip CB on HTTP 4xx + strip orphaned tool messages on resume

- Circuit breaker now ignores HTTP 400/401/403/404 client errors
  (only server errors and transient failures should open the breaker)
- Strip orphaned tool result messages before sending to API,
  preventing HTTP 400 'No tool call found for function call output'
  errors on session resume (when history is partially restored)
- Applied in both chat() and stream() paths

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add comprehensive .gitignore for full-stack application

- ASP.NET Core: bin/, obj/, .vs/, packages/, *.user, *.suo, migrations
- Angular/Node.js: node_modules/, dist/, .angular/, package-lock.json
- IDE settings: .vscode/, .idea/, *.swp, editor configs
- Environment files: .env, appsettings.Development.json (excluding secrets)
- Database files: *.db, SQLite temporary files
- Logs and temp: logs/, *.log, tmp/, .cache
- OS files: .DS_Store, Thumbs.db, desktop.ini
- Backup files: *.bak, *.backup, *.orig
- Generated files: dist/, build/, .tsbuildinfo

Prevents accidental commits of sensitive data, dependencies, and build artifacts.
Covers both backend (.NET) and frontend (Angular) development workflows.

fix: add pinDigest to Renovate rules and schedule automerge at 0530 UTC (#374)

pinDigest is a distinct Renovate update type not covered by pin or
digest. It was missing from all matchUpdateTypes arrays, causing PRs
like #367 and #371 to fall through every rule — no automerge and no
manual-review label.

- Add pinDigest to default labeling, Tier 1, Tier 2, and GitHub
  Actions automerge matchUpdateTypes
- Add automergeSchedule to restrict automerge to 05:30-06:00 UTC daily

commit.message:
fix: listSprints ignores milestone-closed when sprint already in map

Root cause found: listSprints() adds the active sprint to the map
first (from live state with phase='init'). When the milestone loop
runs later, it skips the sprint because it's already in the map —
only adding milestoneNumber but NOT updating the phase. So a closed
milestone's sprint stayed 'init' in the sprint list.

Fix: milestone-closed always overrides phase, even for existing entries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: split compound ptr+i32_to_u64 in substr/substr_len to prevent register clobbering

The expression copy_bytes_raw(ptr(s) + i32_to_u64(start), count) can cause
register clobbering on ARM64 when both ptr() and i32_to_u64() use the same
internal register (x9). Splitting into separate local variables ensures
correct behavior across all compiler versions.

commit.message:
feat: LUKS1 + LUKS2 full in-bootloader encryption support

Implements complete encrypted Linux drive support for UEFI targets:

Crypto layer (crypto/):
- SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512 hash functions
- HMAC-SHA{1,256,512}
- PBKDF2 (RFC 2898)
- Blake2b (RFC 7693) — required by Argon2
- Argon2id/Argon2i/Argon2d (RFC 9106) — LUKS2 KDF
- AES-128/256 block cipher (FIPS 197, table-based)
- XTS-AES and CBC-AES (plain/plain64/ESSIV) disk encryption modes

LUKS layer (luks/):
- LUKS1: header parse, PBKDF2 key derivation, AF-split key material
  decrypt, master key verification
- LUKS2: binary + JSON header parse, Argon2 and PBKDF2 KDF, AF merge,
  PBKDF2-SHA256 digest verification
- luks_vol: unified encrypted volume I/O, on-the-fly AES-XTS/CBC decrypt
- luks_open_efi: EFI_BLOCK_IO_PROTOCOL adapter for direct hardware access

UI:
- common/passphrase: masked passphrase input (shows *, backspace, ESC)

Integration (uefi_loader.c + ext4.c):
- encrypted=yes in sakuru.cfg triggers LUKS unlock before kernel load
- Passphrase prompt with configurable retry count (luks_tries, default 3)
- Optional luks_keyfile path support
- ext4_mount_luks: mounts ext4 directly on top of decrypted LUKS volume
- gBS_alloc_pool/gBS_free_pool helpers for Argon2 memory allocation

Config (common/config.h + config.c):
- New BootEntry fields: encrypted, luks_keyfile, luks_tries
- sakuru.cfg.example updated with encrypted entry example

BIOS target intentionally excluded (size constraints).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SakuruBoot
repository.description: Lightweight UEFI & BIOS bootloader with config-driven OS selection menu (x86_64 + AArch64)

commit.message:
Normalize ./prefix in markdown image path resolution

Strip leading ./ from image URLs before joining with the parent
directory so paths like ./image.png resolve cleanly to dir/image.png
instead of dir/./image.png. Bare filenames like image.png continue
to resolve as local to the markdown file's directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite all 35 speaker notes to concise PURPOSE/KEY POINTS/SEQUENCE format

  SEQUENCE (click/timing guidance where relevant)

Fix PA flow: create-flow in Yes branch of condition

commit.message:
fix: refresh button in header, milestone overrides state everywhere

Three problems fixed:

1. Refresh button moved to header (next to ◀▶ sprint nav buttons)
   - Removed from Issues panel
   - More discoverable, refreshes everything: issues + milestones + phase

2. Milestone-closed overrides state phase at ALL code paths:
   - HTTP /api/sprints/:n/state?refresh=true
   - WebSocket sprint:switch handler
   - WebSocket initial connect handler
   Previously: saved state/live state returned phase='init' even when
   milestone was closed on GitHub. Now milestone-closed always wins.

3. 'viewing — Sprint N running' is now a clickable button that
   navigates back to the active sprint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(adversarial): NEVER pass through rejected output — escalate to higher team

- Replace for/else pass-through with AdversarialEscalation exception
- After MAX_ADVERSARIAL_RETRIES exhausted: NodeStatus.FAILED + raise AdversarialEscalation
- engine.py: propagate AdversarialEscalation like WorkflowPaused (never swallow)
- store.py: catch AdversarialEscalation before generic except, notify RTE/team lead
  via _rte_facilitate(), set run.status='escalated', checkpoint phase for re-run
- CLAUDE.md: fix doc (was 'MAX_ADVERSARIAL_RETRIES=0 — warning only')

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🐛 fix(idempotency): 修正重播 body 格式不一致與分散式測試邏輯

- CaptureResult 改用 IOptions<JsonOptions> 序列化 ObjectResult.Value
  使重播 body 的 JSON 命名規則與原始回應一致（camelCase）
- test:all 加入 api:build 步驟，兩個 pod 共用同一份 build（--no-build）
- 場景 B 兩個 pod 都回傳 201 時，改為比對 ID 是否相同
  ID 相同 → 冪等重播（正確行為），ID 不同 → 鎖失效（真正錯誤）
- 修正 if/fi 結構錯誤

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow 1-space before inline comments in yamllint (#373)

Renovate's GitHub Actions digest pinning produces lines like:
  uses: actions/checkout@SHA # v6
with only 1 space before the comment. The default yamllint
comments rule requires 2 spaces, causing CI failures on
Renovate PRs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
